title: Coverage of essential health services
subtitle: Coverage of essential health services is measured as an index on a scale of 0 to 100 (where higher is better). This index is based on risk-standardized death rates – which give a measure of healthcare access and quality – and whether common interventions are carried out.
caption:
Coverage of essential health services, 2023

Coverage of essential health services is measured as an index on a scale of 0 to 100 (where higher is better). This index is based on risk-standardized death rates – which give a measure of healthcare access and quality – and whether common interventions are carried out.

The map displays the 2023 coverage of essential health services across countries using a color scale ranging from 0 to 100, where darker blue indicates higher coverage and lighter green indicates lower coverage. The index measures healthcare access and quality based on risk-standardized death rates and the implementation of common interventions for promotion, prevention, treatment, and care. High coverage values, represented by dark blue, are visible across North America, including the United States and Canada, as well as throughout Western Europe, including Spain, France, Germany, and the United Kingdom. Australia and Japan also fall into this highest band. In South America, Brazil and Argentina show coverage in the upper-middle range, while countries in Central Asia and parts of Eastern Europe display mid-range values. Lower coverage levels, shown in light green and pale blue, are concentrated across sub-Saharan Africa, including nations such as Nigeria, the Democratic Republic of the Congo, and Somalia. Parts of South Asia, including Afghanistan and Pakistan, also appear in the lower bands of the scale. Greenland and some smaller island nations are marked as having no data available. The distribution shows a clear geographic pattern where high-income regions in the northern hemisphere and Oceania occupy the top of the scale, while many low-income regions in Africa and parts of Asia occupy the bottom.
